instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
GST on Gold Jewellery: Rates, Impact and Calculations
Added Word Count: 484
URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/tax/gst-on-gold.html
The significance of gold in Indian culture and tradition has forever been immense. Gold has been quite popular in most Indian households, be it as an investment, a symbol of status, a luxury expense, and wedding rituals. Since gold is highly valuable to Indians, the government also ensures that it is properly regulated and taxed. GST on gold purchase in India attracts tax on its making and imports. The rates may also differ in different conditions.
Understanding GST on gold
As discussed above, GST on gold can differ in rate depending on the type of gold purchase. It is levied on imports, purchases, and making charges of the gold.
The GST on gold is levied at 3% and this applies to both 22 and 24 carat physical gold. It applies to gold bars, gold jewellery, and even gold coins. So, pure gold in any form attracts 3% of GST. The same rate of 3% also applies to digital gold.
On the other hand, GST on Gold ETFs, gold mutual funds, and sovereign gold bonds may have different GST sets. Gold is not subject to GST, but it may attract management fees, service charges, etc., that may involve 18% GST.
GST calculation on gold purchase and making
GST on gold purchase is applicable when you buy new gold. Additionally, registered gold dealers also have to pay GST on gold.
The GST is applied in the following conditions:
Purchase of new gold
Purchase of new gold attracts 3% GST charges. It may also involve GST on making charges. The gold bar includes 3% GST. As discussed above, this taxation is applicable for both 22 and 24 carat gold purchases.
Purchase of digital gold
Digital gold attracts 3% GST. It includes all the expenses related to insurance premiums, the storage cost, fees of the trustee, etc., similar to that of a physical gold purchase.
GST on making charges
Jewellers charge 3% gold making charge, which is computed on the total price of the product.
Exchange of old gold jewellery
There is no GST applied on the exchange of old gold for new gold jewellery. So, if you have old gold jewellery, you can get it exchanged without having to pay additional GST on it. However, if a registered dealer sells old gold jewellery, 3% GST is levied on it.
GST on import of gold
The import of gold in India attracts 3% GST. It also attracts customs duties. Thus, importing gold in India increases the final price of the gold. The Union Budget 2024 announced a reduction in the customs duty on gold import by 9%. This is the new rate:
GST on gold vs pre-GST tax on gold
The coming of GST definitely increased taxation, so the demand can be seen to have reduced. However, the taxation on gold was different in the pre-GST era. To understand the impact of GST on gold in the current scenario, here is a detailed comparison of the GST and pre-GST on gold:
The tax rates may vary in different states and UTs
From the table above, you can conclude that now the GST rates are slightly higher than the pre-GST rates. Initially, when the GST was introduced, the charges were quite high, which spiked the gold rates. However, the customs duty has now also been reduced, making the overall charges affordable.
E-Way Bill Rules for Gold and Its Different Forms
As per the GST Council regulations, e-way bills have been mandated for the movement of luxury and jewellery within the state and across state borders. Some limitations are set on these transactions:
No changes can be made to the Part B details of the transporter and vehicle after the issue.
It is not allowed to use a consolidated e-way bill, and e-way bills cannot be used to assign more than one vehicle.
Quite oddly, the extension of the e-way bill validity period is possible without the need for Part B details to be changed.
Furthermore, the facility for the disallowance and the annulment of e-way bills as per the standard process also exists for these relocations.
GST Exemptions on Gold
One of the major changes in the Indian tax system is the introduction of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime for gold. Currently 3% GST is levied on both 24-carat and 22-carat gold. Moreover, even after the GST 2.0 reforms, the GST rate on gold jewellery has not changed. It continues to be 3%. Hence, the price of acquiring gold is a sum of the cost of gold and the respective GST rates.
Example of GST Calculation on Gold
Gold buying before the implementation of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) in July 2017 was a very complicated process, as it was subject to various taxes such as value-added tax (VAT), excise duty, and customs duty, which made it difficult even to understand the final price. However, with the introduction of GST, the process has been simplified, and a uniform tax of 3% is levied on the value of gold and 5% on the making charges.
For instance, if gold is available at a rate of ₹60,000 with a making charge of ₹1,500, a combined GST of ₹1,875 (i.e., ₹1,800 for the gold at 3% and ₹75 for the making charges at 5%) will apply. As such, the final price of the gold will be ₹63,375.
Key Takeaways
Purchasing gold in India attracts a rate of 3% GST on the value of gold, plus 5% GST on the making charges.
A similar 3% GST is levied on the online transactions of digital gold, as is the case with physical gold.
No GST is charged on exchanging old gold for new jewellery. However, 3% GST on gold jewellery is levied on sales made by dealers of old gold.
The import of gold is subject to a 3% GST together with cut customs duties, which, in total, determine the final price of gold.
Conclusion
GST, or Goods and Services Tax, was introduced in 2017.2 Since its Introduction, GST has transformed the Indian taxation system to a great extent. Gold, being one of the most valuable and most popular metals in the country, has also been subject to GST. Over the years, the charges have been reduced, as custom duty is now 6%, and gold value GST is 3%.
So, before you step out to purchase or sell gold as an individual or a registered company, it is crucial to stay updated on the GST rules.
FAQs
At what rate is GST charged on new gold jewelry purchases?
GST on new gold purchases is levied at 3%. The charges remain at 3% whether or not the invoice mentions making charges separately.
What is GST on digital gold?
Digital gold attracts 3% Good And Services Tax, just like physical gold.
Is GST applicable on an exchange of old gold with new gold ?
No. The exchange of old gold is not subject to GST charges.
What is the GST rate on gold-making charges?
The GST rate on gold-making charges is 5%, in addition to the 3% GST on the cost of the gold.
Disclaimer
The content provided is for general informational purposes only. The material is compiled from publicly available sources, internal insights, and other information deemed reliable. While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Readers are encouraged to conduct their own due diligence and are advised to seek independent professional or expert advice before making any financial or investment decisions based on the content. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.

output.final_text:
GST on Gold Jewellery: Rates, Impact and Calculations
Added Word Count: 484
URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/tax/gst-on-gold.html
The significance of gold in Indian culture and tradition has forever been immense. Gold has been quite popular in most Indian households, be it as an investment, a symbol of status, a luxury expense, and wedding rituals. Since gold is highly valuable to Indians, the government also ensures that it is properly regulated and taxed. GST on gold purchase in India attracts tax on its making and imports. The rates may also differ in different conditions.
Understanding GST on gold
As discussed above, GST on gold can differ in rate depending on the type of gold purchase. It is levied on imports, purchases, and making charges of the gold.
The GST on gold is levied at 3% and this applies to both 22 and 24 carat physical gold. It applies to gold bars, gold jewellery, and even gold coins. So, pure gold in any form attracts 3% of GST. The same rate of 3% also applies to digital gold.
On the other hand, GST on Gold ETFs, gold mutual funds, and sovereign gold bonds may have different GST sets. Gold is not subject to GST, but it may attract management fees, service charges, etc., that may involve 18% GST.
GST calculation on gold purchase and making
GST on gold purchase is applicable when you buy new gold. Additionally, registered gold dealers also have to pay GST on gold.
The GST is applied in the following conditions:
● Purchase of new gold
Purchase of new gold attracts 3% GST charges. It may also involve GST on making charges. The gold bar includes 3% GST. As discussed above, this taxation is applicable for both 22 and 24 carat gold purchases.
● Purchase of digital gold
Digital gold attracts 3% GST. It includes all the expenses related to insurance premiums, the storage cost, fees of the trustee, etc., similar to that of a physical gold purchase.
● GST on making charges
Jewellers charge 3% gold making charge, which is computed on the total price of the product.
● Exchange of old gold jewellery
There is no GST applied on the exchange of old gold for new gold jewellery. So, if you have old gold jewellery, you can get it exchanged without having to pay additional GST on it. However, if a registered dealer sells old gold jewellery, 3% GST is levied on it.
● GST on import of gold
The import of gold in India attracts 3% GST. It also attracts customs duties. Thus, importing gold in India increases the final price of the gold. The Union Budget 2024 announced a reduction in the customs duty on gold import by 9%. This is the new rate:
Custom Duty Gold Bar and Findings Gold Dore
Old New Old New
Basic customs duty 10% 5% 10% 4%
AIDC (Agriculture Infrastructure and Development Cess) 5% 1% 4.35% 0.35%
Total 15% 6% 14.35% 5.35%
GST on gold vs pre-GST tax on gold
The coming of GST definitely increased taxation, so the demand can be seen to have reduced. However, the taxation on gold was different in the pre-GST era. To understand the impact of GST on gold in the current scenario, here is a detailed comparison of the GST and pre-GST on gold:
Components of tax Pre-GST rates
GST rates
Assumed Base Price (10gm gold) 1,00,000 1,00,000
Service Tax of 1% 1,000 Nil
VAT (Assessable Value) 1,01,000 1,00,000
VAT (1%) 1,010 Nil
GST (Assessable Value) - 1,00,000
GST of 3% Nil 3,000
Total Gold Value 1,02,010 1,03,000
The tax rates may vary in different states and UTs
From the table above, you can conclude that now the GST rates are slightly higher than the pre-GST rates. Initially, when the GST was introduced, the charges were quite high, which spiked the gold rates. However, the customs duty has now also been reduced, making the overall charges affordable.
E-Way Bill Rules for Gold and Its Different Forms
As per the GST Council regulations, e-way bills have been mandated for the movement of gold and other precious stones within the state and across state borders. Some limitations are set on these transactions:
1. Changes to Part B of the e-way bill, which contains the details of the transporter and vehicle,
cannot be updated by default. 2. It is not allowed to use a consolidated e-way bill, and e-way bills cannot be used to assign
more than one vehicle. 3. The extension of the e-way bill validity period is possible without the need for Part B details
to be changed. However, the facility for rejecting or cancelling an e-way bill is available as per the standard process when moving gold and precious stones.
GST Exemptions on Gold1
One of the major changes in the Indian tax system is the introduction of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime for gold. Currently 3% GST is levied on both 24-carat and 22-carat gold. Moreover, even after the GST 2.0 reforms, the GST rate on gold jewellery has not changed. It continues to be 3%. Hence, the price of acquiring gold is a sum of the cost of gold and the respective GST rates.
Example of GST Calculation on Gold2
Gold buying before the implementation of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) in July 2017 was a very complicated process, as it was subject to various taxes such as value-added tax (VAT), excise duty, and customs duty, which made it difficult even to understand the final price. However, with the introduction of GST, the process has been simplified, and a uniform tax of 3% is levied on the value of gold and 5% on the making charges.
For instance, if gold is available at a rate of ₹60,000 with a making charge of ₹1,500, a combined GST of ₹1,875 (i.e., ₹1,800 for the gold at 3% and ₹75 for the making charges at 5%) will apply. As such, the final price of the gold will be ₹63,375.
Key Takeaways
1. Purchasing gold in India attracts a rate of 3% GST on the value of gold, plus 5% GST on the
making charges2. 2. A similar 3% GST3 is levied on the online transactions of digital gold, as is the case with
physical gold. 3. No GST3 is charged on exchanging old gold for new jewellery. However, 3% GST on gold
jewellery is levied when the dealer resells the old gold jewellery . 4. The import of gold is subject to a 3% GST3 together with cut customs duties, which, in total,
determine the final price of gold.
Conclusion
GST, or Goods and Services Tax, was introduced in 2017.2 Since its Introduction, GST has transformed the Indian taxation system to a great extent. Gold, being one of the most valuable and most popular metals in the country, has also been subject to GST. Over the years, the charges have been reduced, as custom duty is now 6%, and gold value GST is 3%.
So, before you step out to purchase or sell gold as an individual or a registered company, it is crucial to stay updated on the GST rules.
FAQs
1. At what rate is GST charged on new gold jewelry purchases?
GST on new gold purchases is levied at 3%. The charges remain at 3% whether or not the invoice mentions making charges separately.
2. What is GST on digital gold?
Digital gold attracts 3% Good And Services Tax, just like physical gold.
3. Is GST applicable on an exchange of old gold with new gold ?
No. The exchange of old gold is not subject to GST charges.
4. What is the GST rate on gold-making charges?
The GST rate on gold-making charges is 5%, in addition to the 3% GST on the cost of the gold2.
